body_preview:
Jason, we're bringing in a new brake rotor line from the Mexico plant. Want your read on the HTS classification before the first entry. Specs attached.

Ray
subject: HTS classification on the new brake rotor line

body_preview:
Ray, looks like 8708.30 to me on first read, but let me run it against the casting note before we commit. Hold the entry until I confirm.

Jason
subject: Re: HTS classification on the new brake rotor line

body_preview:
Understood. Holding. Thanks.

Ray
subject: Re: HTS classification on the new brake rotor line

body_preview:
Confirmed 8708.30. You're clear to file. I'll send the classification memo for your records.

Jason
subject: Re: HTS classification on the new brake rotor line

body_preview:
Jason, any word from CBP on our binding ruling request for the stamped housings?

Karen
subject: Great Lakes binding ruling status

body_preview:
Not yet. They're running about 45 days right now. I'll push for an update next week and let you know the day it lands.

Jason
subject: Re: Great Lakes binding ruling status

body_preview:
Appreciate it. We need it before the Q2 shipments.

Karen
subject: Re: Great Lakes binding ruling status

body_preview:
We'll have it in time. If it slips I'll file under the conservative classification so you're not held up.

Jason
subject: Re: Great Lakes binding ruling status

body_preview:
Jason, the housing supplier switched to a Mexican source. Does that change our USMCA call on SN-4420?

Ray
subject: USMCA origin recheck on sensor housing SN-4420

body_preview:
It can. Send me the bill of materials and the supplier's origin docs and I'll rerun the regional value content before you sign anything.

Jason
subject: Re: USMCA origin recheck on sensor housing SN-4420

body_preview:
Sending the BOM now.

Ray
subject: Re: USMCA origin recheck on sensor housing SN-4420

body_preview:
Karen, your ISF on the Hamburg container is due Thursday. I'm still missing the consolidator's name. Can you get it to me by tomorrow noon?

Jason
subject: ISF deadline this week

body_preview:
On it. I'll have it to you this afternoon.

Karen
subject: Re: ISF deadline this week

body_preview:
Got it, filed. You're clear.

Jason
subject: Re: ISF deadline this week

body_preview:
Jason, we had a batch of rotors come back. Can we claim drawback on the duty we paid?

Ray
subject: Duty drawback on the returned rotors

body_preview:
Likely yes, if they're exported or destroyed under CBP supervision. Send me the entry numbers and the disposition and I'll build the claim.

Jason
subject: Re: Duty drawback on the returned rotors

body_preview:
Jason, can we set a call to go over the Q1 entries? Want to make sure nothing's open.

Karen
subject: Q1 entry summary review

body_preview:
Sure. Mornings are better for me. How's the 12th at 9?

Jason
subject: Re: Q1 entry summary review

body_preview:
9 on the 12th works. Thanks.

Karen
subject: Re: Q1 entry summary review

body_preview:
Jason, heard there's a tariff change coming on steel parts. Does it hit our castings?

Ray
subject: Tariff increase on steel components

body_preview:
Some of them. I'll pull your part list and flag which lines are exposed and by how much. You'll have it by Friday.

Jason
subject: Re: Tariff increase on steel components

body_preview:
Thanks, that helps with pricing.

Ray
subject: Re: Tariff increase on steel components

body_preview:
Karen, the reconciliation entry posted. CBP accepted the value adjustment, no penalty. I'll send the closeout.

Jason
subject: Reconciliation entry follow-up

body_preview:
That's a relief. Thank you for staying on it.

Karen
subject: Re: Reconciliation entry follow-up
